Operation 'get_/users/{user-id}/followed-by' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Description lacks security scope requirements and doesn't clarify that this returns followers (incoming relationships). Added pagination context and security requirements for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Get the list of users this user is followed by.
data.current_description: Get the list of users this user is followed by. To get users followed by the owner of the access token, you can use **self** instead of the `user-id`.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the list of users who follow the specified user (incoming followers). To get followers of the authenticated user, use **self** instead of the `user-id`. Returns a paginated list of user objects. Requires authentication and `follower_list` scope to access private accounts.
data.reasoning: Description lacks security scope requirements and doesn't clarify that this returns followers (incoming relationships). Added pagination context and security requirements for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'get_/users/{user-id}/follows' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Description lacks security scope requirements and pagination context. Added clarity about outgoing relationships, security requirements, and return format to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Get the list of users this user follows.
data.current_description: Get the list of users this user follows. To get follows of the owner of the access token, you can use **self** instead of the `user-id`.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the list of users that the specified user follows (outgoing follows). To get the authenticated user's follows, use **self** instead of the `user-id`. Returns a paginated list of user objects. Requires authentication and `follower_list` scope to access private accounts.
data.reasoning: Description lacks security scope requirements and pagination context. Added clarity about outgoing relationships, security requirements, and return format to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'get_/users/{user-id}/relationship' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description is too minimal and lacks specificity about what relationship information is returned. Added details about return values (follow status, requests, blocking), authentication requirements, and use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Get information about a relationship to another user.
data.current_description: Get information about a relationship to another user.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the relationship status between the authenticated user and the specified target user. Returns relationship information including follow status, incoming/outgoing follow requests, and blocking status. Requires authentication. Use this to determine if the authenticated user follows the target user or if there are pending follow requests.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal and lacks specificity about what relationship information is returned. Added details about return values (follow status, requests, blocking), authentication requirements, and use cases to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'post_/users/{user-id}/relationship' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is extremely vague with no details about inputs, outputs, or side effects. Added specificity about the action parameter options, return value, authentication requirements, and state change implications. This significantly improves AI agent ability to determine when and how to use this operation.

data.current_summary: Modify the relationship between the current user and the target user.
data.current_description: Modify the relationship between the current user and the target user.
data.summary_suggestion: Modify relationship status with another user.
data.description_suggestion: Modifies the relationship between the authenticated user and the specified target user. Accepts an `action` parameter to follow, unfollow, block, unblock, approve, or deny follow requests. Returns the updated relationship status. Requires authentication and appropriate security scopes. This operation changes the authenticated user's relationship state with the target user.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague with no details about inputs, outputs, or side effects. Added specificity about the action parameter options, return value, authentication requirements, and state change implications. This significantly improves AI agent ability to determine when and how to use this operation.

Original description is repetitive and lacks detail. Improved version clarifies the search parameter, specifies what is returned (tag objects with name and media count), and provides context for when an AI agent would use this operation.

data.current_summary: Search for tags by name.
data.current_description: Search for tags by name.
data.summary_suggestion: Search for tags by name query
data.description_suggestion: Search for tags matching a specified name query string. Returns a list of tag objects containing tag name and media count. Useful for discovering hashtags and tag popularity before filtering media by tag.
data.reasoning: Original description is repetitive and lacks detail. Improved version clarifies the search parameter, specifies what is returned (tag objects with name and media count), and provides context for when an AI agent would use this operation.

message: Operation 'get_/tags/{tag-name}' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details. Original description is too generic. Improved version specifies what information is returned (tag name, media count), clarifies the input (tag-name path parameter), and explains the practical value of the returned data for AI agents.

data.current_summary: Get information about a tag object.
data.current_description: Get information about a tag object.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve detailed information about a specific tag including the tag name and the total count of media associated with that tag. Returns a tag object with metadata useful for understanding tag popularity and reach.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic. Improved version specifies what information is returned (tag name, media count), clarifies the input (tag-name path parameter), and explains the practical value of the returned data for AI agents.

data.current_summary: Search for a user by name.
data.current_description: Search for a user by name.
data.summary_suggestion: Search for users by name query
data.description_suggestion: Search for Instagram users matching a specified name query string. Returns a list of user objects containing user ID, username, full name, and profile picture URL. Supports partial name matching to help discover user accounts.

data.current_summary: Get recent media from a custom geo-id.
data.current_description:
Get recent media from a geography subscription that you created.

**Note:** You can only access Geographies that were explicitly created by your OAuth client. Check the
Geography Subscriptions section of the [real-time updates page](https://instagram.com/developer/realtime/).
When you create a subscription to some geography that you define, you will be returned a unique `geo-id` that
can be used in this query. To backfill photos from the location covered by this geography, use the
[media search endpoint](https://instagram.com/developer/endpoints/media/).

**Warning:** [Deprecated](http://instagram.com/developer/changelog/) for Apps created **on or after** Nov 17, 2015
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve recent media from geography subscription
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves recent media objects from a geography subscription that you created. You can only access geographies explicitly created by your OAuth client. The geo-id is returned when you create a geography subscription. Returns a list of media objects (images and/or videos) from the geographic area. Note: This endpoint is deprecated for apps created on or after November 17, 2015. For backfilling historical photos, use the media search endpoint instead.
data.reasoning: The current description is verbose and unclear about what is returned. Added clarity on: (1) what the operation returns (media objects), (2) media types supported, (3) deprecation status and its implications, (4) relationship to media search endpoint. Improved summary to be more action-oriented and specific about the geography subscription context.

data.current_summary: Search for media in a given area.
data.current_description:
Search for media in a given area. The default time span is set to 5 days. The time span must not exceed 7 days.
Defaults time stamps cover the last 5 days. Can return mix of `image` and `video` types.
data.description_suggestion: Searches for media objects within a specified geographic area and optional time range. Requires geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude) and optional min/max timestamp parameters. Default time span is 5 days; maximum time span is 7 days. Returns a list of media objects (images and/or videos) matching the search criteria, ordered by recency. Useful for discovering content from specific locations and time periods.

data.current_summary: Get information about a media object.
data.current_description:
This endpoint returns the same response as `GET /media/{media-id}`.

A media object's shortcode can be found in its shortlink URL. An example shortlink is
`http://instagram.com/p/D/`, its corresponding shortcode is `D`.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve media information using shortcode identifier
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a media object using its shortcode instead of media ID. The shortcode is the alphanumeric identifier found in Instagram shortlink URLs (e.g., shortlink http://instagram.com/p/ABC123/ has shortcode ABC123). Returns the same media object response as the GET /media/{media-id} endpoint, including media metadata, captions, timestamps, and engagement data. Useful when you have a shortlink URL but need to access media details programmatically.
data.reasoning: The current description is unclear about what information is returned and why you would use this endpoint.
